summary.intent: Fix three P2-severity issues in a deterministic compaction feature for a chat system, then commit and push to trigger the next review round.
summary.outcome: All three P2 fixes were implemented (mode-specific label in compaction notice, totalTokens/contextWindow in compact broadcast, WARN log for missing provider fallback), passed fmt/clippy/tests, and were pushed as round 5 fixes.

repo: The project uses a worktree-based workflow under /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/ for feature branches, with iterative review rounds (round 5, round 6).
Compaction logic is split between crates/chat/src/lib.rs (ChatService, broadcast, formatting) and crates/chat/src/compaction_run/mod.rs (dispatcher/strategy logic).
The RecencyPreserving compaction mode has a fallback to a default 200K context window when no LLM provider is passed by the caller.
LlmProvider and model context are imported from moltis_agents and must be explicitly brought in when adding token/context metadata to broadcasts.

code.finding: format_channel_compaction_notice was hardcoding 'deterministic strategy' instead of using a mode-specific label; fixed to use dynamic label.
ChatService::compact broadcast for 'chat.compact done' was missing totalTokens and contextWindow fields; added after importing moltis_agents model types.
Dispatcher for RecencyPreserving mode silently fell back to 200K context window when provider was absent; added WARN log to surface this condition.

workflow: The team uses a round-based iterative review process where fixes are batched per round, committed, pushed, and then a poll/trigger kicks off the next round.
Validation sequence is: cargo fmt → clippy + tests → no-default-features clippy, all must be green before committing.
Multiple P2 issues are addressed in a single pass/commit rather than one commit per fix.

friction: Needed multiple file reads and bash searches to locate the correct functions (format fn, LlmProvider import, moltis_agents usage) before making edits, indicating the codebase lacks obvious naming conventions for quick navigation.

The existing test for recency_preserving_fallback_honestly only checked a generic substring ('No LLM tokens used'), requiring an explicit additional assertion to cover the new mode-specific label behavior.
open_items: Round 6 review is pending — more fixes may be identified after the push.

The 200K default context window fallback in RecencyPreserving mode is now logged as a WARN but the underlying issue (callers not passing a provider) may need a longer-term fix to enforce provider presence.
